name: Cordoba (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the Caribbean Sea and the Sinu river that drain the department
the peace, purity and integrity of the Cordobese people
the fertile cattle-raising savannahs and agricultural lands of Cordoba

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of blue, white, and green.
history: Cordoba was created as a department on 18 June 1951, separating from Bolivar. Its flag's tricolour reflects the natural setting of the department: sea and rivers, the peace of its people, and the fertility of its plains.

features.symbolism: blue for the Caribbean Sea and the rivers, white for peace and purity, green for the fertile cattle-raising plains and savannahs
